title: UA Professors Read Their Own Reviews on Rate My Professor
body: I think one of the downsides of Rate My Professor is that sometimes students will say a professor is way too hard, when it's actually not the professor at all. sometimes the subject is actually hard to understand for that student. I absolutely suck at anything related to physics, but my physics teachers never were horrible, it was just a hard concept for me to understand.

body: The only reviews I take with a grain of salt are “the class is really hard.” Some classes are hard, that doesn’t necessarily mean the professor is bad. title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris
body: i'm only about halfway through the video but had to pause to say this-- you have no obligation to share your dv story with anyone if you don't feel ready. if someday you feel that sharing will be healthy and beneficial for you then that's wonderful!! but if that day never comes there is absolutely nothing wrong with that. don't feel like you have to get to the point of sharing publicly someday, everyone's journey and healing process looks different and there's never a requirement to share something so personal on such a public platform. thank you for being vulnerable and sharing even the pieces that you have shared. you are strong and brave and powerful and nobody can take that away from you!
author: @megsnicolec

title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris

title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris
body:
I don't know why I'm re-watching this, all these years later, as someone waaaay too old for rush, but here I am. And I just have to say I am so glad you went back to your beautiful brunette hair - it really gives you so much more visual depth. Been here from the beginning and it is always because you have been authentic.

Oh, and what if you could tell this Lauren that you're living your NYC dream and ENGAGED!!!!
author: @shellastew

title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris
body: This was so fun to watch.  I rushed 50 years ago! I have a funny story about Pi Phi at my university from during my experience.  it was during the 2nd round of parties, and they served us dishes of rice pudding.  The girl who was my hostess kept gushing about how this was a special thing that everyone in the house loved, and that their cook made it all the time.  Meanwhile, I am gagging it down, trying to be polite.  I am not a rice fan, and in this pudding, it was disgusting to me.  Now the members seemed all so kind, and the house was very pretty, but I dropped them because the thought of being served this rice pudding "all the time" was something I couldn't see living with, LOL!   Then something else funny happened at KD during that same round of parties.  It was warm inside the house, and I had removed my coat.  I had a great conversation with my hostess and had a good time.  Near the end of the party, she went to assist me with getting my coat on.  My hand got tangled in the sleeve, and I had to push really hard to get it through.  In doing so I slugged her in the face!  It was an accident of course, and I felt so terrible, as I really clocked her.  I apologized profusely, and she seemed to laugh it off.  I got dropped before the next set of parties.  Gee, I wonder why?😂
author: @thekirksiffs5285

title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris
body: I definitely understand how being dropped by the sorority that had a philanthropy about DV was upsetting when you reacted to it. If they did/do hands on types of service they may have made that judgement to separate you from people who also need healing. I survived through DV as well and I know for a long time I couldn't have been as helpful because I needed to heal past my trauma first. I definitely understand how you felt in the moment though, but it does look like you landed in the right place in either case and seemed to have a positive experience. :)

author: @Ms.Conner26

title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris
body: I was 33 minutes in before you shared that you started and went through rush at age 21, and that was the age I was, and I remember everyone in my house and pledge class making such a huge deal about me being older and how uncomfortable that made me. I think they were either excited or afraid that I was old enough to legally drink. Anyway I got dropped from my #1 house every round, but I did get a bid and I was so excited for that. I made the best of my years there and my alumni years were even better, holding leadership positions in every city I lived post-college. So I guess the point I want to make for everyone reading this is that NO MATTER WHAT house you get, you will have so many opportunities by going greek, and not just for 4 years but 4 LIFE! So don't be afraid to take the lowest house on your ranking. You will love the girls in your pledge class and you will be so proud to be a member there!
author: @a-muse6567

title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris
body: My college was heavily Greek.  No sorority houses.  Rush is spring Freshman year.   I was not upset when I was dropped across the board.  I was FURIOUS with how my best friend was treated both times she rushed.  I ended up in a co Ed service fraternity. Far cheaper, more welcoming and was the right choice for me.

Thank you for sharing your experience so candidly. This was really helpful.
author: @awesomemeg

title: Rushing at The University of Alabama | Name Dropping, What Houses Dropped Me, & More | Lauren Norris
body:
Thank you for sharing so openly!!
A few things from a person with close to NO sorority life experience!
-I KNEW something about Zeta would be SUS
-that girl actually said to you “I’m not allowed to let you leave so we’re just going to stand here”?!??????!!!!!!!!! Are you kidding me. Fail. She should be ashamed of herself.
-Conversely, the girl who said “if you don’t see yourself here that’s np, let’s just talk about whatever” = A++++++++ this was literally such kindness and class
-I’m sorry to hear about the domestic violence history. Uncontrollable panic and sobbing is such a difficult experience let alone in that context. It makes sense that you were hurt when they dropped you because it’s so unexpected and out of your expectation based on their previous behavior. I’d guess it was genuine but that other girls didn’t vote /you didn’t get enough votes from other girls to come back
-What does it mean for someone to be an “academic liability” to a sorority? I understand gpa cutoffs but what the heck is a liability - and that seems so unreasonable and obnoxious

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: Thank you for talking about the predatory nature of the parties. I went to a frat party once and it was terrifying . They literally didn’t allow anyone to come inside except women and actually threw me out eventually because I refused to drink. It was in an incredibly secluded place .. I got the impression that all of these dudes had joined a frat so that they could have access to women in these types of scenarios where women are the only people invited, forced to drink, and then kind of in a position where they can’t easily leave because there’s no public transportation and they basically end up staying over to either have consensual or non-consensual sex .

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: As a woman of color who grew up in the South, sorority/fraternity culture is still so uncomfortably weird to me especially considering how Greek life culture/politics within universities bleeds into the public society and government. While these academic societies are great to help create different communities on campus, they are known to have a lot of corruption and shouldn’t have that power to negatively impact students and the general public.

body: My mom was a member of Delta Gamma at USC in the 80s. Her sorority didn't have a house because at the time, more than 4 unmarried women living in a house together was considered a brothel.
author: @Ivy-od1qu

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body:
Not my story, but I'm English, and my best friend (who's a dude) went to Orlando for a placement year at uni.
Apparently him, and every single one of his friends he made in the US, fucking HATED frat guys. They also basically stopped being friends with someone because he joined a fraternity and just became a completely different person.
The general idea I got from him was that they are just full of really dodgy, arrogant, entitled and downright toxic guys. Also at frat parties they would be so candid about how they exploit the women and ply them with alcohol (or worse) to coerce them into sex and how that was the 'norm' for a lot of them because they expect to 'get laid'. It was absolute horrifying how nonchalant they were about committing SA.
So yeah, fraternities sound like an absolute patriarchal horror story.
author: @mrhappyjuice

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: I went into this expecting racist white girls with bad spray tans and it ended with mafia-esque political corruption 😭
author: @hecklingpickle7696

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body:
As someone who is black and English and went to an American university in the south, I remember I rushed and ultimately didn’t get selected for any sororities. However, after rush was over and I would see these girls at frat parties, and had multiple tell me on separate occasions that the reason they could accept me was because they either already had a black girl or they were holding out for the only other black girl that rushed (coincidentally someone I went on to be great friends with) and if they had too many black girls in the house then frats wouldn’t invite them to parties anymore.
It was also interesting just being black at frat parties because no one would talk to me until they heard my accent and then suddenly and miraculously it was like I wasn’t black anymore.
author: @stephaniehibbert7335

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: The reason you’re not allowed to talk about drinking is that all the girls rushing are freshman who can’t legally drink and the older girls need to at least pretend be secretive about allowing underage drinking
author: @padkrapaoenthusiast

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: I cleaned over the summers at a SUNY school I went to and found a girl's notebook and sorority docs. There were fees between $50-$500 that had to be paid if you gained weight, weren't early enough to meetings, tagged in 'bad' pictures on social media, etc. The notebook was just filled to the brim with her logging every single thing she did and tracking all of her dieting and workout stuff and it was for the sorority to look over. So messed up.
author: @7thStrongest

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: I’d also like to mention the popular girl to sorority pipeline in the US is SO prevalent. Girls I went to high school with were in these giant friend groups that were getting invited to upperclassmen parties, but only certain girls were invited by certain seniors. By the time we were upperclassmen, they were the ones throwing the parties, inviting FRESH freshmen. And even within their friend groups, there was pledging and a need for multiple outfits, hair and makeup done, and an expensive car. My best example is when these girls had beach week, and each day of the week had a different theme, and this one day was USA out. I remember so well being with these girls while they were purchasing their swimsuits, and most of the girls already owned red white and blue bikinis, but a girl bought three new sets (two in the same colour) just because she had ANOTHER beach week with another USA theme. And when I asked why she didn’t just wear the same one, she said it was because of pictures. Anywho, each and every one of those girls are in a sorority at a large state school. Each and every one of them.
author: @queenv22

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: i just want to say as a black woman, the divine 9 are a group of historically black sororities and fraternities and the rush process for these are EXTREMELY DIFFERENT than other greek life. they provide w higher sense of community and some even work on battling the disadvantaged black people in america face. they are culturally different as well including step routines and strolls. most of the other greek societies are now trying to implement this too and it’s becoming quite controversial
author: @lol.8794

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: My personal theory on why the documentary is disappointing, is that they were actually going to delve a LOT deeper, but when the rumors about filming started and the area became actively dangerous for the crew, they had to dial WAY back and ended up really softening the entire documentary and taking a new angle when in post to reduce backlash as much as they could. But that's just my theory, I went in really wanting to like the documentary.
author: @cirruscloud5198

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: someone died at my university during frat hazing, they made a freshman chug a couple full bottles of hard liquor and then left him to die essentially. the frat's no longer allowed on our campus, even unofficially
author: @hiphillbert

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: You know this makes a lot of sense as to why adults that were in sororities act the way they do.
author: @tiaslays255

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body:
I think something that’s not talked about enough in the whole “joining a sorority” process is the aftermath of actually receiving a bid. Speaking from my own experience, the girls in my pledge class and I weren’t just immediately welcomed with open arms after bid day. They literally made us work for the title of “sister,” explicitly stating multiple times that we aren’t part of the sisterhood until we receive our letters. Its this weird power-hungry dynamic that felt like they were feeding off the fact that we weren’t on the same level as them yet.

Needless to say, I dropped like a week after I got my bid. The whole process just felt so secretive and “icky,” for a lack of better words.
author: @ilusia.

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: Although the idea of a sorority can sound like "sisterhood and community", it honestly looks eery to me when that community is primarily and selectively made out of rich, White, cis women. Like its not really sisterhood when only the most privileged women can be a part of it and it feels like a "Get Out" scenario if you're a marginalized person who was let in.
author: @sapphic.flower

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: I think this is the first video I’m seeing as an American citizen where Bamarush and Sorority/Fraternity culture is being analyzed from a non-American perspective. I’d really love to see more people talk about this through a non-American lens so I can see what their perspectives bring to the conversation.
author: @mfuentes4961

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body:
So for context, I'm from Florida and I go to the University of Florida. During covid, I did a "soft" rush in the Spring (which is a much gentler process than during the fall term), and I still struggled so much in the appearance and conversations. I got a bid by the 'lowest' sorority and felt no sense of community between the two girls who talked to me about their sorority. When I got the proposed dues I simply knew that it was impossible (even though I had mentally already decided not to join any longer). I also realized that the reason I got the bid was because this sorority was in the process of building their house and they simply needed as many funds as possible to make it happen.

Hazing is definitely still a thing. I go to the 'lowest' tier frat parties and I still hear some minor hazing that is definitely nothing compared to the horror stories you hear, don't involve alcohol, and are usually just plain silly to be fair. There's even hazing for sororities because I heard of this thing that PNMs are supposed to decide in one of the top sororities (I believe Tri Delt but I could be wrong), where they have to 'blow or blow'. They are told to either do a line of coke (Tri Delt is also known as the coke sorority to be fair) or blow someone (I don't know whether it's in front of the women, if men are present, or if they have to 'prove' it somehow) but all of it is disgusting.

There's such an economic gap between the feasibility of sororities for lower to middle-class students versus those that are from upper-middle class or the 1%. It is completely unaffordable and insane to think about. Even though U Florida is NOT Alabama and is a little bit more liberal, every year the divine 9 monument gets defaced. The girls look the exact same (majority white) and the aesthetic is all Vera Bradley and wealthy. Being a brunette is considered diversity. The rush process is so similar here to Alabama that I just have to imagine any large 'public' school has a similar process. I had heard of the 2/3 rule at sororities here where they either have to have hair, makeup and nails, or their fit all done to be at events. You get fined if you miss a meeting or an event and fined like ten times more for missing anything within the fall rush process.

Pike (or Phi Kappa something –– not epsilon surprisingly) is known under the nomer 'Pike spikes' and IFC (the inter-fraternity council) continually tries to monitor some things but because it's mostly run by frat brothers, there's not that much that gets under control. Every year, a fraternity gets in trouble for sexual assault allegations. My ex, who was in the 'lowest' tier frat (regularly gets made fun of by the other frats) had to walk lil freshers to all the frat houses for their meetings with the frat brothers trying to pledge them, and Pike specifically used their 'sexual prowess' as a means of trying to get men to join. They are genuinely using the toxic frat culture to try to appeal to men who will uphold the same toxicity. In the US we have a thing called 'sign night' where, on your 21st birthday when you can finally drink, you go out with girlfriends and wear a sign that has 21 things to do before the evening ends. Usually there's something about kissing strangers, signing people, getting plastered, etc. and Pike mentioned that it's the easiest to 'get' with these girls on their 21st sign night because they will usually have something sexual on their sign and also be quite drunk. Pike at UF actually has an entire wall in their mansion filled with photographs of women on their sign night. When they were getting this tour, there were women actively hanging out in the frat as visual evidence to show the brother's sexual prowess. INSANE!!!! And I am not involved in any of these groups except for having some male friends in the socially 'LOWEST' frats (that still hear about these things).

Sorry this is so long, but I figured I could add some of my two-sense being in a technically Southern state with a massive greek life participating university. Politically they have so much power. It's only until recently that there's been an opposition group to student government that attempts to undo some of this corruption, but even then, they haven't gotten very far. Until a publication published something on it, students in greek life were forced to safe their voting sticker to prove they voted to receive their dinner! If they didn't have one or didn't vote, they wouldn't get FOOD! They technically don't have to prove what group they voted for, but it's assumed they are supporting their 'sisters' or 'brothers'. Greek life is also a means of career connections because there are all of these privileges you'll receive for having been in the same greek life group as someone else who is now a head CEO or career-person (they can offer you jobs, internships, make you look more desirable in the hiring process). It is genuinely seen as putting yourself back by not being a part of these groups to establish professional connections that will help you FOR LIFE.
author: @cassieglover

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: I'm a housemom at a sorority. I've been doing this for 15 years and we are in no way chaperones. The ladies who live in our houses are adults. We will say something if they are disregarding a house rule but that is it. Our role is property management, overseeing the staff, and being here in case of emergencies. The sorority is self governing and we do not get involved in their behavior beyond be respectful of the house, your sisters and the staff. If they need someone to talk to, I am always available but only if they want to talk. I am a motherly figure to them but not in the way that I tell them what to do. I will give advice if they ask or privately let them know if I am concerned about a behavior if I think they are struggling so they know I'm there if they need me. But if there are issues that is addressed by the Honor Board, I am in no way involved in discipline or keeping tabs on their comings and goings.
author: @BubblesLuvsYa81

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body:

Edit: spelling + i commented this at the halfway point of the video and it's even more terrifying now that i finished it. Not just the racism (that should be an obvious problem) but just how cultlike the whole system is. Holy shit

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: One of my acquaintances from high school ended up going to Alabama State. [TW: hazing resulting in severe bodily injury] According to him, he started noticing a lot of freshman guys with broken arms on campus during the fall. He was told that it was a result of hazing from Frats. Apparently, they will have pledges put their arms between two mattresses and leave it there while the older Frat members JUMP on the top mattress. It's technically hearsay and could definitely be a rumor, but the fact that it was even a rumor is wild. There are no rumors like that at my school, so some pretty wild hazing must have happened to at least inspire a rumor that extreme. It seemed like the hazing was just an open secret that never got addressed because the victims were groomed into becoming perpetrators themselves later on.
author: @bad_bau

title: The Cult of Sororities & BamaRush
body: If you think sororities are crazy, look up cotillion and debutante (in Georgia specifically the company Social Inc.) Starting at 11 years old, kids take weekly classes learning ballroom dancing until the age 17/18. This grooming doesn't start in college, it starts in middle school.

title: UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA *CAMPUS TOUR* | Classrooms, Sorority Row, Stadium, Starbucks, Library, Dorms
body:
Many things changed, since I was a student at UoA (1977-1984), the ΔΔΔ house was across the street from where it is now and it was round.
Barnwell field, that we used to play soccer is now a parking lot.
But the ΔΓ anchor, even with it's new brass color (it was dark colored in my days) reminded me of a time that our ΘΤ (back then it was a men's fraternity and ΔΓs were our Little Sisters, I think this changed now because in the new form ΘΤ became coed because is was a professional engineering fraternity and I believe the old scheme was dissolved for a while) pledges hand cuffed me on the anchor and as our members were chasing them around, in order to get the handcuff's key to free me, they lost the key in the grass lol.
I got so mad with the whole thing, that I put the double side of the cuff my hand was tied with, over a blade near the top of the ΔΓ anchor and using my arm as a lever, I broke the pin of the hand cuffs and set my self free, with only minor cuts on the sides of my wrist.
So the ΔΓ anchor has it's history with me too lol.
author: @FLORATOSOTHON

title: MOVING INTO MY COLLEGE APARTMENT! | University of Alabama | apartment tour & move in day vlog
body: I'm old but love watching move-in videos. I am in the process of re-doing my bedroom and have gotten a lot of ideas from people. I have all of the furniture except the mattress. It is coming in a few weeks. I just find these kinds of videos so fun and remind me of my younger days. I hope you have an amazing year. EDIT: My husband put together all of the furniture for the bedroom and I am surprised he didn't divorce me LOL. He is a good guy and didn't complain even once. One of the reasons for re doing the room was storage. There were so many drawers to put together. I got some Alex drawers, the tall ones and two dressers with 9 drawers each. The vanity has 7 drawers. Poor guy.

title: MOVING INTO MY COLLEGE APARTMENT! | University of Alabama | apartment tour & move in day vlog
body:
Please, everyone wash your sheets before using. I used to stick at Target many years ago and the boxes they came in hard a warning label. The sheets themselves don’t have the warning label.

Sheets—especially cotton sets—that are labelled as 'wrinkle-free', 'no wrinkle' or 'no iron' typically contain a formaldehyde finish, which can cause a sore throat, teary eyes, blocked sinuses, a runny nose and sneezing.
author: @carolineyawale

title: MOVING INTO MY COLLEGE APARTMENT! | University of Alabama | apartment tour & move in day vlog
body: When traveling with jewelry open your necklace, slide it through a piece of a straw and re-close the necklace no more tangles. If your storing several together in a drawer, this is also a great idea.

title: SORORITY AND FRATERNITY ROW | The University of Alabama Campus Tour | Part 2
body: Thank you so much for sharing these videos about U of Alabama! I found them very helpful. My son was accepted there and is trying to make a decision where to attend in the fall as a freshman. title: Jain University, Jayanagar - Campus Tour & Honest Student Experience | Kavach Khanna
body:
The location of Jain Jayanagar campus is great! It's centrality in Bangalore and it is surrounded by some malls, great restaurants and cafeterias. The only drawback is there's no sports facility due to space constraint.

I am a Jainite btw
author: @preetaexoxo376

title: Take a Tour of UAB's Campus!
body: I'm currently a junior in high school and I've been college searching since I was a freshman. I've become very very fond of UAB, and I've actually made this my top school choice, aka, my dream school. I just love the beautiful campus, the history of the city, and the fact it's everything I've been looking for in a school. When I'm feeling down, I'll literally look up pictures of the campus and think "wow. I will probably be here in two years." it's incredible to think about. I'm excited to be a blazer! (hopefully, fingers crossed!) 💚💛
author: @jasminevines2883

title: Take a Tour of UAB's Campus!
body: I love it! I really hope that I am able to get the opportunity to attend UAB in the fall of 2021.
author: @alexandrasavostikova6023

title: Take a Tour of UAB's Campus!
body: My family and i visited the campus this weekend (just because it's close and we're that kind of family lmao), and I have to admit that it really impressed me. We saw everything featured in this video, but we also saw the fitness (?) sort of place, complete with that little gym and even a huge swimming pool. So amazed by the beauty and peacefulness of the campus as well (or at least when we visited)! If you are going to go off to college soon or just looking at them out of pure interest (me), I would definitely recommend this place. Later on, I hope to apply and get in to this university as well!
author: @moonionrings

title: University of Greenwich Campus Tour 2026 — Honest Student Reviews from SE10 London
body: Hi Anushka, Your videos are very helpful. I am coming from Non technical background and I have close to 4 year of experience as a working professional. I have applied for msc big data and business intelligence course, since I'm working into analytics and also certified by Google for data analytics. My consultant said that they cannot accept my profile but I have relevant experience as working into IT and I have seen candidates studying there for postgraduate data science and similar course from non technical background. Can you please help with some suggestions here. I have applied for Jan 2022 intake.
